message:
fix(fcp-zapier): clear handshake state on re-configure (br-2ot4f cross-crate audit)

Found during a fresh-eyes pass on br-2ot4f focused on cross-crate
integration boundaries. The connector lifecycle in zapier (and the
same shape exists in other instantiated connectors per br-2ot4f
comment 39 about the generator template) leaves stale handshake
state on re-configure:

Before: handle_configure replaces self.client and self.config with
new values and sets set_configured(true), but does NOT touch
set_handshaken or session_id. After a re-configure (e.g.,
credential rotation or a hostile re-config under the same connector
instance), the connector reports `handshaken = true` against a
session_id that was negotiated under the OLD auth/base_url. The
host may have already invalidated that session, and any subsequent
invoke routes through the NEW client with stale session metadata.

Fix: handle_configure now clears self.session_id = None and calls
self.base.set_handshaken(false) BEFORE installing the new
client/config. The connector must complete a fresh handshake under
the new auth before invokes can succeed. Mirrors the discipline
the generator template was tightened to in br-2ot4f comment 39.

Regression test (lifecycle_reconfigure_invalidates_handshake):
establishes a full lifecycle, re-configures with a fresh api_key,
asserts health reports configured=true + handshaken=false +
status=degraded.

256 fcp-zapier tests passing (208 lib + 48 integration; +1 new).

Other connectors with the same shape (handle_configure setting
configured but not clearing prior handshake) likely need the same
fix — out of scope for this commit; tracked as a follow-up bead.
